---
title: "rddj-template"
subtitle: "My analysis"
author: "My Name"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    code_folding: show
    echo: TRUE
    warning: FALSE
    message: FALSE
    highlight: pygments
    theme: paper
    df_print: kable
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 4
    number_sections: yes
    toc_float: 
      collapsed: yes
      smooth_scroll: false
# knit: (function(inputFile, encoding) {
  # rmarkdown::render(inputFile, encoding = encoding, output_dir = "../", output_file = "index") })
# favicon: img/favicon.ico
# url: "https://walker-data.com/census-r"
# cover-image: "04-visualizing-census-data_files/figure-html/visualize-error-bars-1.png"
# description: "This book is an overview of how practitioners can acquire, wrangle, visualize, and model US Census data with the R programming language."
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
## By default, show code for all chunks in the knitted document,
## as well as the output. To override for a particular chunk
## use echo = FALSE in its options.
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
   echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE
)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
# CONFIG
user_name <- "fernandomillanvillalobos" # your Git username (only needed if
# you want to deploy to GH pages)
project_name <- "rddj-template" # adapt!
r_version <- "4.6.1" # R-Version to use
options(Ncpus = 4) # use 4 cores for parallelized installation of packages
if (r_version != paste0(version$major, ".", version$minor)) {
  stop("ERROR: specified R version does not match currently used.")
}
```

# Notes

This report was generated on `r Sys.time()`. R version: `r paste0(version$major, ".", version$minor)` on `r version$platform`. Package versions used for this report are pinned in the project's `renv.lock` file, so the exact same versions can always be restored with `renv::restore()`.

...

## R-Script & data

The preprocessing and analysis of the data was conducted in the [R project for statistical computing](https://www.r-project.org/). The RMarkdown script used to generate this document and all the resulting data can be downloaded [under this link](http://`r user_name`.github.io/`r project_name`/). Through executing `main.Rmd`, the herein described process can be reproduced and this document can be generated. In the course of this, data from the folder `input` will be processed and results will be written to `output`. The html on-line version of the analysis can be accessed through this [link](https://`r user_name`.github.io/`r project_name`/). 

## GitHub

The code for the herein described process can also be freely downloaded from [https://github.com/`r user_name`/`r project_name`](https://github.com/`r user_name`/`r project_name`). 

## License

...

## Data description of output files

#### `abc.csv` (Example)

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| a | Numeric | ... |
| b | Numeric | ... |
| c | Numeric |  ... |

...

# Set up

This project uses [renv](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/) to pin exact package versions instead of relying on a CRAN snapshot service. If you're cloning this repo for the first time, run `renv::restore()` once (or just knit this document, which does it for you) to install the versions recorded in `renv.lock`. If this is a brand-new project with no `renv.lock` yet, run `renv::init()` once instead -- see the README for the full setup and package-update workflow.

```{r, echo=FALSE}
detach_all_packages <- function() {
  basic_packages_blank <-  c("stats",
                             "graphics",
                             "grDevices",
                             "utils",
                             "datasets",
                             "methods",
                             "base")
  basic_packages <- paste("package:", basic_packages_blank, sep = "")

  package_list <- search()[
    ifelse(unlist(gregexpr("package:", search())) == 1, TRUE, FALSE)]

  package_list <- setdiff(package_list, basic_packages)

  if (length(package_list) > 0)  for (package in package_list) {
    detach(package, character.only = TRUE, unload = TRUE)
    print(paste("package ", package, " detached", sep = ""))
  }
}

detach_all_packages()

# this allows multiple persons to use the same RMarkdown without adjusting
# the working directory by themselves all the time. here::here() finds the
# project root by looking for a .Rproj file (or a .here file) and resolves
# all paths relative to it, so this replaces the old csf()/setwd() dance
if (!requireNamespace("here", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("here")
}
here::i_am("analysis/main.Rmd") # assumes main.Rmd sits at the project root --
# update this path (e.g. "analysis/main.Rmd") if you move it into a subfolder

# suppress scientific notation
options(scipen = 999)

# suppress summarise info
options(dplyr.summarise.inform = FALSE)
```

## Define packages

```{r, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# if you don't need a package, remove it from here (commenting not
# sufficient) -- this vector is also what gets restored/loaded below.
# adding a package? add it here, install.packages() it as usual, then run
# renv::snapshot() to record the new version in renv.lock (see README)
# tidyverse: see https://blog.rstudio.org/2016/09/15/tidyverse-1-0-0/
packages <- c(
  "rstudioapi",
  "tidyverse",
  "tidylog",
  "jsonlite",
  "lintr",
  "rmarkdown",
  "rio",
  "cowplot",
  "extrafont",
  "ggrepel",
  "scales",
  "janitor"
)
```

## Install packages

```{r, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# this project uses renv (https://rstudio.github.io/renv/) instead of
# checkpoint/MRAN to keep an isolated, reproducible package library.
# - brand-new project, no renv.lock yet? run renv::init() once, manually,
#   from the console -- that scans the packages above and records their
#   current versions in renv.lock (commit it, renv/activate.R and
#   renv/settings.json afterwards; renv/library/ stays untracked)
# - cloned a project that already has renv.lock? nothing to do by hand,
#   the line below restores those exact versions into a project-local
#   library automatically, no CRAN snapshot server required
# prompt = FALSE keeps this non-interactive so knitting never blocks on a
# y/n prompt; it only installs/updates packages that are actually out of
# sync with renv.lock, it won't touch or remove anything else in your
# library. drop the argument (or set it to interactive()) if you'd rather
# review changes before they're applied
if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("renv")
}
renv::restore(prompt = FALSE)
```

## Load packages

```{r, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
invisible(lapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))
sessionInfo()
```

## Load additional scripts

```{r, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# if you want to outsource logic to other script files, see README for 
# further information
# Load all visualizations functions as separate scripts
knitr::read_chunk(here::here("scripts", "dviz.supp.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "dviz.supp.R"))
knitr::read_chunk(here::here("scripts", "themes.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "themes.R"))
knitr::read_chunk(here::here("scripts", "plot_grid.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "plot_grid.R"))
knitr::read_chunk(here::here("scripts", "align_legend.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "align_legend.R"))
knitr::read_chunk(here::here("scripts", "label_log10.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "label_log10.R"))
knitr::read_chunk(here::here("scripts", "outliers.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "outliers.R"))
```

# Introduction
# Data Importing
# Data Pre-Processing
# Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
## Data Summarization
## Data Visualization

# Linting

The code in this RMarkdown is linted with the [lintr package](https://github.com/jimhester/lintr), which is based on the  [tidyverse style guide](http://style.tidyverse.org/). 

```{r echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='hide'}
# lintr::lint(here::here("main.Rmd"), linters =
#               lintr::with_defaults(
#                 commented_code_linter = NULL,
#                 trailing_whitespace_linter = NULL
#                 )
#             )
# if you have additional scripts and want them to be linted too, add them here
# lintr::lint(here::here("scripts", "my_script.R"))
```
